commands.review_summary:
The branch hardens Gateway MCP loopback tool schema projection and tool calls so malformed or unreadable tool metadata is omitted or rejected while healthy sibling tools remain usable.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +378. Total +476 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main directly reads/spreads loopback tool metadata and directly compares tool names during calls, while the branch adds synthetic tests for throwing getters and malformed union schemas. I did not run the failing pre-patch test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This is a protected maintainer-labeled draft with compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior and no narrow automated repair target.

target.title: fix(gateway): harden MCP loopback tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardens MCP loopback tool schema projection so malformed union variants and unreadable tool metadata do not break `tools/list` or let invalid tools execute.
- Keeps healthy loopback tools callable when neighboring plugin-owned tool rows have unreadable names, descriptions, or parameters.
- Intentionally scoped to Gateway MCP loopback schema/call handling. Broader app-server and plugin-registry fuzz guards remain out of scope.

## Linked context

Related #87141

This is a pruned critical-hardening slice from the larger tool-schema fuzz branch.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: malformed MCP loopback tool schemas and unreadable loopback tool metadata are isolated instead of poisoning the full tool list/call path.
- Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper plus AWS Crabbox changed gate.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/mcp-http.handlers.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: focused Gateway test passed 1 routed shard / 27 tests at head `da5efc4e707661a37c921d6864b16086893bf7ec`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; scrub returned no matches; AWS Crabbox `run_021006f8b58f` on lease `cbx_3d9108b92bfd` passed `pnpm check:changed` with lanes `core` and `coreTests`.
- Observed result after fix: malformed union property schemas are skipped or merged safely, boolean union variants preserve optionality, unreadable schema rows are omitted from advertised tools, and `tools/call` refuses tools that were not safely advertised.
- What was not tested: a live external MCP client session. Coverage uses synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures against the Gateway MCP loopback test server.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none for the touched contract path.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/mcp-http.handlers.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Regression coverage added:

- malformed MCP union property schemas preserve healthy schema variants
- boolean union variants preserve optionality and do not hide later usable schemas
- unreadable loopback names, descriptions, and parameters are isolated
- calls cannot execute tools omitted from the advertised schema list

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed Gateway MCP loopback tool metadata is now omitted/rejected instead of breaking or executing through an unsafe row.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Tool execution through the loopback Gateway now requires the tool to be present in the safely projected schema list.

What is the highest-risk area?

Gateway MCP loopback compatibility for malformed plugin-owned tool schema metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The behavior is scoped to unreadable or malformed rows. Healthy tools remain listed and callable, and the changed gate plus focused loopback server tests cover the call path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local structured review reported no accepted/actionable findings. Remote changed gate initially found test type gaps; the final pushed head fixes them and passes `pnpm check:changed` remotely.
